mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is positive when policy rates are declining from prior readings and loses power if rates are flat or rising.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling versus the prior month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is actively accelerating rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 20-day forward SPX returns more often than not, or if average triggered return is not at least 1 percentage point below unconditional mean over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise together, household income and retail demand are improving simultaneously, which should support near-term equity gains through stronger expected revenues. The signal is not valid if sales rise while payroll growth is contracting.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to outperform single-series cases, or if 5-day SPX returns are negative in a majority of joint-up instances over a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO combined with rising HOUST indicates real-sector output is expanding without an immediate demand squeeze, which should support S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The mechanism fails when industrial output improves but housing collapses, signaling tightening financial conditions instead.
failure_condition: Reject if positive co-movement in INDPRO and HOUST does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling 3-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens discount rates and increases recession risk, which should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is already elevated, indicating restrictive policy is binding.
failure_condition: Reject if months with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than months below the threshold, or if the effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend signals payroll contraction and weaker aggregate income, which should weigh on equities over the next month through weaker consumption expectations. The signal fails when payroll declines occur alongside strong retail sales growth, which offsets labor weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines do not correspond to below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal vanishes when evaluated only on periods with flat or declining RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 confirms broad real-demand growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter by raising expected corporate revenues. The signal is strongest when GDP growth is accelerating versus the previous quarter and fails if output growth is decelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly changes in GDPC1 do not predict higher 60-day SPX returns than negative changes, or if the rolling 3-year slope of the relationship is consistently indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in INDPRO and HOUST marks a tightening real-economy impulse that should weaken S&P 500 returns over the next week as cyclical earnings expectations are revised down quickly. The signal is negative only when both indicators are deteriorating together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls and HOUST falls in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in INDPRO and HOUST are not followed by below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is weaker than either series alone in out-of-sample tests.
